## CONTENT_BLOCK

### Summary
Answers to common questions about pothole reporting, repair timelines, and compensation.

### Questions and Answers
- Q: How do I report a pothole?
  A: Call 0800 1234, send a WhatsApp to 0712 345 678, or use the Harare City Connect app. Provide the exact location and a photo if possible.

- Q: What information do I need to provide?
  A: Street name, suburb, nearest landmark, approximate size (e.g., “30 cm wide, 10 cm deep”), and a photo.

- Q: How long does it take to fix a pothole?
  A: Emergency potholes (dangerous, deep, on highways) are usually fixed within 48 hours. Standard potholes within 2 weeks, depending on weather and asphalt availability.

- Q: Can I track the status of my report?
  A: Yes – use your reference ID on the Harare City Connect app or call the hotline and ask for an update.

- Q: Can I claim compensation for vehicle damage caused by a pothole?
  A: Yes, but you must prove the council was notified and failed to act within a reasonable time. Contact the Legal Services department at townclerk@harare.gov.zw with your reference ID, photos, and repair invoice.

- Q: What if the pothole is on a road managed by the central government (e.g., A1 highway)?
  A: Report it to the Department of Roads (ZINARA) instead. For most urban roads in Harare, the council is responsible.
